claim: Major vault protein (MVP) functions to decrease tumor aggression.

result.claim: Major vault protein (MVP) functions to decrease tumor aggression.
result.reasoning: The claim that Major vault protein (MVP) functions to decrease tumor aggression is refuted by evidence from paper 19800147, "MVP-mediated exosomal sorting of miR-193a promotes colon cancer progression". This paper states that "MVP-mediated selective sorting of tumour suppressor miRNA into exosomes promotes tumour progression." It further explains that "Knockout of MVP leads to miR-193a accumulation in the exosomal donor cells instead of exosomes, inhibiting tumour progression." This indicates that MVP's action of sorting tumor suppressor miRNA into exosomes actually facilitates tumor progression, and its absence inhibits it, directly contradicting the idea that MVP decreases tumor aggression.

evidence.text: In KRAS mutant tumors, PIK3CA mutation restores Cyclin D1 expression and G1/S cell cycle progression.
evidence.source: PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling

evidence.text: Cav-1 functions as a tumor suppressor in the stromal microenvironment.
evidence.source: An Absence of Stromal Caveolin-1 Expression Predicts Early Tumor Recurrence and Poor Clinical Outcome in Human Breast Cancers

evidence.text: Physcion inhibited 6PGD, cancer cell proliferation and tumor growth.
evidence.source: 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase links oxidative PPP, lipogenesis and tumor growth by inhibiting LKB1-AMPK signaling

evidence.text: Stromal Cav-1 expression was scored in a breast cancer tissue microarray.
evidence.source: An Absence of Stromal Caveolin-1 Expression Predicts Early Tumor Recurrence and Poor Clinical Outcome in Human Breast Cancers

evidence.text: MDSCs increase TIC frequency through activating Notch in tumor cells.
evidence.source: Oncogenic mTOR signaling recruits myeloid-derived suppressor cells to promote tumor initiation

evidence.text: BCL-2 is an antiapoptotic protein.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: S3 inhibited 6PGD, cancer cell proliferation and tumor growth.
evidence.source: 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase links oxidative PPP, lipogenesis and tumor growth by inhibiting LKB1-AMPK signaling

evidence.text: Abrogating G-CSF production diminished tumor growth.
evidence.source: Tumor-Derived G-CSF Facilitates Neoplastic Growth through a Granulocytic Myeloid-Derived Suppressor Cell-Dependent Mechanism

evidence.text: miR-193a interacts with major vault protein (MVP).
evidence.source: MVP-mediated exosomal sorting of miR-193a promotes colon cancer progression

evidence.text: The determination of suppression of tumorigenesis involves Kinesin superfamily proteins (KIFs).
evidence.source: Intracellular Transport and Kinesin Superfamily Proteins, KIFs: Structure, Function, and Dynamics

evidence.text: Nlrp12 inhibits T cell proliferation.
evidence.source: The Dual Immunoregulatory function of Nlrp12 in T Cell-Mediated Immune Response: Lessons from Experimental Autoimmune Encephalomyelitis

evidence.text: HRDE-1 directs gene-silencing events in germ-cell nuclei.
evidence.source: A nuclear Argonaute promotes multi-generational epigenetic inheritance and germline immortality

evidence.text: mTOR signalling in cancer cells regulates G-CSF.
evidence.source: Oncogenic mTOR signaling recruits myeloid-derived suppressor cells to promote tumor initiation

evidence.text: Monoclonal antibodies against the ectodomain of N-cadherin reduced proliferation, adhesion, and invasion of prostate cancer cells in vitro.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: Physcion and S3 inhibited tumor growth in nude mice xenografts.
evidence.source: 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase links oxidative PPP, lipogenesis and tumor growth by inhibiting LKB1-AMPK signaling

evidence.text: mTORC2 is a critical regulator of amino acid metabolism in cancer.
evidence.source: mTORC2 Regulates Amino Acid Metabolism in Cancer by Phosphorylation of the Cystine-Glutamate Antiporter xCT.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited the activation of Akt, S6 ribosomal protein, and 4EBP1 in breast cancer cells.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: Ectopically over-expressing G-CSF in G-CSF-negative tumors significantly augmented tumor growth.
evidence.source: Tumor-Derived G-CSF Facilitates Neoplastic Growth through a Granulocytic Myeloid-Derived Suppressor Cell-Dependent Mechanism

evidence.text: VEGF directly and negatively regulates tumor cell invasion through enhanced recruitment of the protein tyrosine phosphatase 1B (PTP1B) to a MET/VEGFR2 heterocomplex.
evidence.source: VEGF inhibits tumor cell invasion and mesenchymal transition through a MET/VEGFR2 complex.

evidence.text: MVP-mediated selective sorting of tumour suppressor miRNA into exosomes promotes tumour progression.
evidence.source: MVP-mediated exosomal sorting of miR-193a promotes colon cancer progression
